# Steps

A step-by-step process indicator component for multi-step workflows such as wizards, onboarding flows, or checkout processes. Built with composable sub-components.

## Import

```jsx
// Nested property pattern (recommended)
import { Steps } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";

// Use as: Steps.List, Steps.Item, Steps.Trigger, etc.

// Or individual named imports (legacy)
import {
  Steps,
  StepsList,
  StepsItem,
  StepsTrigger,
  StepsIndicator,
  StepsSeparator,
  StepsContent,
  StepsTitle,
  StepsDescription,
  StepsNextTrigger,
  StepsPrevTrigger,
  StepsCompletedContent,
} from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Sub-components

| Nested Property | Named Export (Legacy) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Steps` | `Steps` | Root container that manages step state |
| `Steps.List` | `StepsList` | Container for the step indicators |
| `Steps.Item` | `StepsItem` | Individual step indicator |
| `Steps.Trigger` | `StepsTrigger` | Clickable trigger for navigating to a step |
| `Steps.Indicator` | `StepsIndicator` | Visual indicator (number, icon, checkmark) for each step |
| `Steps.Separator` | `StepsSeparator` | Visual connector line between steps |
| `Steps.Content` | `StepsContent` | Content panel for each step |
| `Steps.Title` | `StepsTitle` | Title text for a step |
| `Steps.Description` | `StepsDescription` | Description text for a step |
| `Steps.NextTrigger` | `StepsNextTrigger` | Button to advance to the next step |
| `Steps.PrevTrigger` | `StepsPrevTrigger` | Button to go back to the previous step |
| `Steps.CompletedContent` | `StepsCompletedContent` | Content shown when all steps are completed |

## Props

### Steps (Root)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `variant` | `"solid" \| "subtle"` | `"solid"` | The visual style variant |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "md" \| "lg"` | `"md"` | The size of the steps |
| `orientation` | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` | `"horizontal"` | The layout orientation |
| `defaultStep` | `number` | `0` | The initially active step (zero-indexed) |
| `step` | `number` | - | Controlled active step |
| `onStepChange` | `(details: { step: number }) => void` | - | Callback when the active step changes |
| `count` | `number` | - | Total number of steps |
| `linear` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether steps must be completed in order |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the underlying element |

### StepsItem

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `index` | `number` | - | The zero-based index of this step (required) |

### StepsContent

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `index` | `number` | - | The zero-based index of the step this content belongs to (required) |

## Usage

### Basic

```jsx
<Steps defaultStep={0} count={3}>
  <Steps.List>
    <Steps.Item index={0}>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
        <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
      </Steps.Trigger>
      <Steps.Separator />
    </Steps.Item>

    <Steps.Item index={1}>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
        <Steps.Title>Details</Steps.Title>
      </Steps.Trigger>
      <Steps.Separator />
    </Steps.Item>

    <Steps.Item index={2}>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
        <Steps.Title>Confirmation</Steps.Title>
      </Steps.Trigger>
    </Steps.Item>
  </Steps.List>

  <Steps.Content index={0}>Create your account</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.Content index={1}>Fill in your details</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.Content index={2}>Review and confirm</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.CompletedContent>All steps completed!</Steps.CompletedContent>
</Steps>
```

### With descriptions

```jsx
<Steps defaultStep={0} count={3}>
  <Steps.List>
    <Steps.Item index={0}>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
        <div>
          <Steps.Title>Account Setup</Steps.Title>
          <Steps.Description>Create your credentials</Steps.Description>
        </div>
      </Steps.Trigger>
      <Steps.Separator />
    </Steps.Item>
    {/* more items */}
  </Steps.List>
  {/* content */}
</Steps>
```

### With navigation buttons

```jsx
<Steps defaultStep={0} count={3}>
  <Steps.List>
    {/* step items */}
  </Steps.List>

  <Steps.Content index={0}>Step 1 content</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.Content index={1}>Step 2 content</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.Content index={2}>Step 3 content</Steps.Content>
  <Steps.CompletedContent>Done!</Steps.CompletedContent>

  <Group>
    <Steps.PrevTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="outline" size="sm">Previous</Button>
    </Steps.PrevTrigger>
    <Steps.NextTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="outline" size="sm">Next</Button>
    </Steps.NextTrigger>
  </Group>
</Steps>
```

### Vertical orientation

```jsx
<Steps defaultStep={0} count={3} orientation="vertical">
  <Steps.List>
    <Steps.Item index={0}>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
        <Steps.Title>Step 1</Steps.Title>
      </Steps.Trigger>
      <Steps.Separator />
    </Steps.Item>
    {/* more items */}
  </Steps.List>
  {/* content */}
</Steps>
```

## Features

- Supports horizontal and vertical orientations
- Built-in navigation with Next/Previous trigger components
- `Steps.CompletedContent` for displaying content after all steps are done
- Visual indicators with numbers/icons and separator lines between steps
- Supports controlled and uncontrolled step state
- Optional linear mode to enforce sequential completion
- Supports multiple sizes and visual variants
- Each step can have a title and description
- Forwards refs on all sub-components

## API Pattern

This component uses the **nested property pattern** for better organization and discoverability:

```jsx
// Recommended: Use nested properties
<Steps>
  <Steps.List>
    <Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Indicator />
      </Steps.Trigger>
    </Steps.Item>
  </Steps.List>
</Steps>

// Legacy: Individual named imports still supported for backward compatibility
import { StepsList, StepsItem, StepsTrigger } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

The nested property pattern provides:
- Better IDE autocomplete when typing `Steps.`
- Clear component relationships
- Reduced import statements
- Backward compatibility with existing code
